content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File                                                    Date  :  Jan. 9, 1981


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION              A-81-01-002
           Sarah Cameron

Subject: Insurance companies as "commercial lending institutions"


          At the Conflicts of Interest Seminar at Los Angeles on
January 7, 1981 I advised in response to a question that insurance
companies are commercial lending institutions within the meaning
of Section 82030(b)(8)(B), i.e., that the $10,000 reporting
threshold applies to loans one gets on one's insurance policies.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether insurance companies qualify as 'commercial lending institutions' under Section 82030(b)(8)(B) for purposes of the $10,000 reporting threshold for loans.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Insurance companies are commercial lending institutions within the meaning of Section 82030(b)(8)(B), and therefore the $10,000 reporting threshold applies to loans obtained on insurance policies.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found; advice was given orally at a seminar and documented in memo form. Question and conclusion synthesized from body text. Document appears to be an internal memo confirming advice given rather than a formal response letter.

QUESTION: Whether insurance companies qualify as 'commercial lending institutions' under Section 82030(b)(8)(B) for purposes of the $10,000 reporting threshold for loans.

CONCLUSION: Insurance companies are commercial lending institutions within the meaning of Section 82030(b)(8)(B), and therefore the $10,000 reporting threshold applies to loans obtained on insurance policies.
